# **HPG 6104 – Epidemiological Methods I (2025-2026)**

Welcome to the course repository for **HPG 6104: Epidemiological Methods I** at the University of Nairobi. This repository houses all the interactive course materials, student handouts, and web-based calculators designed to support the 2025-2026 academic year.

🌐 **Access the Live Course Portal here:** [https://mndiritu.github.io/hpg6104\_2025-2026/](https://mndiritu.github.io/hpg6104_2025-2026/)

## **📖 Overview**

The goal of this project is to move beyond static PDFs and PowerPoint presentations by providing Epidemiology students with interactive, browser-based tools that reinforce core concepts. The materials focus on applied public health math, study designs, and data interpretation.

## **🗂️ Course Content Directory**

### **Week 1: Measures of Disease Frequency & Association**

* **Course Portal (index.html)**: The main landing page for students to navigate weekly materials.  
* **Student Handout (week1\_handout.html)**: A printable, mobile-friendly quick-reference guide covering the 2×2 table, prevalence, incidence, relative risk (RR), and odds ratios (OR).  
* **2×2 Epi Calculator (epi\_calculator.html)**: An interactive tool where students can plug in ![][image1] values to instantly calculate and interpret risk, RR, OR, and Attributable Risk.  
* **Practice Quiz (week1\_quiz.html)**: A self-paced, auto-grading quiz that tests students on denominator selection and epidemiological metrics.

### **Upcoming Modules**

* **Week 2**: Categorical Data Analysis & Contingency Tables (Chi-square, Fisher’s exact, Mantel–Haenszel)  
* *(Additional weeks will be added as the semester progresses)*

## **🛠️ Technology Stack**

These tools are built to be lightweight, fast, and accessible on any device (including mobile phones with poor internet connections).

* **HTML5 & CSS3**  
* **Tailwind CSS** (via CDN for rapid styling)  
* **Vanilla JavaScript** (for interactive calculators and quiz logic without heavy framework dependencies)

## **🎓 For Educators & Contributors**

If you wish to reuse or modify these materials for your own courses:

1. Clone the repository.  
2. Open index.html in your browser.  
3. No build tools or server environments are required—everything runs natively in the browser.

*Developed for educational use. University of Nairobi.*
